description: Trigger and behavior cases for docs-first implementation: lockfile version pinning, version-matched doc reading, declared uncertainty, and real verification.

cases.prompt: Add server-side pagination to the users table using our data-grid library.

assertions: Identifies the exact installed version of the data-grid library from the lockfile, not the package.json range.
Consults documentation matching that version (versioned docs, installed package files, or bridged changelog) and records which source was used.
Summarizes only the task-relevant API facts before implementing, and follows any existing repo wrapper/pattern for the library.
Runs the project's real test/build/lint commands and reports exact commands with actual results.

cases.prompt: Integrate the payments SDK v0.3.1 we have pinned — their docs site only covers v1.x now.

assertions: Attempts to bridge via the installed package's own README/types/source and the changelog between v0.3.1 and v1.x.
States remaining uncertainty explicitly as unverified assumptions with mitigations, instead of implementing v1.x syntax from memory.
Presents the upgrade-vs-proceed-with-risk option to the human if the gap is unbridgeable.

cases.prompt: Rename the internal helper formatCurrency to formatMoney and update its three call sites.

assertions: Pure project-internal change touching no external API; no version-pinning ritual is needed.

cases.prompt: Use the ORM's new bulk-upsert API to speed up the import job.

assertions: Discovers from the pinned version's docs whether bulk-upsert exists in the installed version.
If the API only exists in a newer version, stops and surfaces the required dependency bump as its own approval-gated change instead of silently upgrading or writing code that cannot run.
Does not implement against the newer API while the old version is installed.
